intake_questions.text: Does the organisation have a documented Incident Response Plan (IRP)?
Has the IRP been tested (tabletop exercise or live drill) in the last 12 months?
Is there a Security Operations Centre (SOC) or equivalent monitoring function?
Are incident severity levels and escalation paths formally defined?
Has the organisation experienced a significant security incident in the last 24 months?

scope_template.prompt: Identify which teams are involved in incident detection, response, and recovery
List systems considered critical for incident scope classification
Confirm incident types or systems explicitly excluded from this assessment

requirements.text: A documented, management-approved Incident Response Plan must exist covering roles, responsibilities, detection, classification, containment, eradication, recovery, and post-incident review.
requirements.evidence_expectation: IRP document with version control; management approval; role assignments; escalation matrix; contact directory

requirements.text: Mechanisms for detecting security incidents must be in place including SIEM/monitoring, alert thresholds, and 24/7 coverage aligned to NIST DE.CM.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Detection tool inventory; SIEM configuration; monitoring coverage matrix; on-call schedule; alert ruleset documentation

requirements.text: Containment and eradication procedures must be documented per incident type with defined authorisation levels for containment actions.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Containment playbooks by incident type; authority matrix for containment actions; isolation procedures

requirements.text: The IRP must be tested through tabletop exercises or live drills at least annually, with lessons learned documented and acted upon.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Tabletop exercise plan and results (within 12 months); lessons-learned report; IRP update evidence post-exercise

requirements.text: Notification procedures must cover regulatory reporting obligations (72-hour GDPR/DPDP notification), internal escalation, and external communication including clients and insurers.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Notification procedure; regulatory reporting templates; stakeholder communication matrix; cyber insurance policy excerpt

evidence_requests.title: Incident Response Plan
evidence_requests.description: Current IRP document with version history, management approval, role assignments, and escalation matrix

evidence_requests.title: Detection Capability Evidence
evidence_requests.description: SIEM/detection tool documentation, monitoring coverage matrix, on-call schedule, and sample alert log

evidence_requests.title: Containment Playbooks
evidence_requests.description: Incident-type playbooks for at least ransomware, data breach, and account compromise scenarios

evidence_requests.title: Most Recent Tabletop Exercise Report
evidence_requests.description: Tabletop exercise scenario, participant list, findings, and lessons-learned implementation evidence

evidence_requests.title: Notification Procedures and Templates
evidence_requests.description: Regulatory notification templates, stakeholder communication matrix, and cyber insurance evidence

task_templates.title: Review IRP for completeness against NIST SP 800-61 framework
Assess detection and monitoring capability coverage
Evaluate containment playbooks for key incident types
Assess tabletop exercise quality and lessons-learned implementation
Review notification procedures for regulatory compliance
Interview IR team lead on detection-to-containment process

finding_templates.title_pattern: IRP not tested within required period; last test: {last_test_date}

severity_model.critical: No documented or tested IRP; inability to detect or respond to incidents; regulatory notification failure risk
severity_model.high: Significant IR capability gap; key incident type without playbook or detection coverage absent
severity_model.medium: IR process weakness; playbooks incomplete or tests overdue; notification gaps present
severity_model.low: Minor IR improvement; playbooks need refresh or tabletop scenario coverage should be broadened
severity_model.informational: Best-practice enhancement for IR maturity; no immediate capability risk

advisory_clinic_templates.title: IRP Design and Update Workshop
advisory_clinic_templates.guidance: Walk through IRP against NIST SP 800-61; identify gaps; update roles, playbooks, and escalation matrix

advisory_clinic_templates.title: Tabletop Exercise Facilitation
advisory_clinic_templates.guidance: Design and facilitate a scenario-based tabletop exercise; produce findings and lessons-learned report

advisory_clinic_templates.title: Detection Engineering Advisory
advisory_clinic_templates.guidance: Review SIEM detection rules against MITRE ATT&CK; recommend high-priority detections for immediate gaps
